2606.27679.title: From Signals to Transfer: A Factorised Study of Probe-Based Uncertainty Estimation in Large Language Models
2606.27679.paper_summary: Probe-based uncertainty estimation (UE) has emerged as a prominent approach to detect hallucinations in Large Language Models (LLMs) by learning uncertainty from internal model signals. Yet, recent methods vary simultaneously across feature design, training data construction, and evaluation setting, obscuring what actually drives performance. To address this issue, we propose a factorised study of probe-based UE under matched conditions. Our results show that raw hidden states and attention features are difficult to outperform in-domain. However, under distribution shift, structured and compressed features are more robust, suggesting that in-domain performance alone is insufficient to measure progress. Furthermore, prompting and label construction significantly affect probe behaviour. Building on these best-practice findings, we train benchmark-based pretrained probes that transfer reasonably well to open-ended factual generation, providing a stable off-the-shelf baseline. Our work encourages more deployment-oriented evaluation of probe-based uncertainty estimators. The code repository is available at https://github.com/ponhvoan/ProbeUE.
2606.27679.paper_summary_zh: 探測器基於的不確定性估計（UE）已成為檢測大型語言模型（LLMs）中幻覺的顯著方法，通過學習來自內部模型信號的不確定性。然而，最近的方法在特徵設計、訓練數據構建和評估設置上同時存在變化，模糊了實際驅動性能的因素。為了解決這個問題，我們提出在匹配條件下對探測器基於的UE進行分解研究。我們的結果顯示，原始隱藏狀態和注意力特徵在領域內難以被超越。然而，在分佈轉移下，結構化和壓縮特徵更具穩健性，這表明僅依賴於領域內性能不足以衡量進展。此外，提示和標籤構建對探測器行為有顯著影響。基於這些最佳實踐的發現，我們訓練了基於基準的預訓練探測器，這些探測器在開放式事實生成中轉移效果相當良好，提供了一個穩定的現成基準。我們的工作鼓勵對基於探測器的不確定性估計器進行更多以部署為導向的評估。代碼庫可在 https://github.com/ponhvoan/ProbeUE 獲得。
2606.27679.author: Ponhvoan Srey et.al.
2606.27679.authors: Ponhvoan Srey, Xiaobao Wu, Cong-Duy Nguyen, Quang Minh Nguyen, Duc Anh Vu, Anh Tuan Luu
